routed_to_questions.q_text: When you think about Key Accounts & Channels specifically — what 2-3 things are eating the most leadership / FTE time?
routed_to_questions.reasoning: Tightly coupled with the trade-promo signal. Manu sees pricing-decision + cross-channel impact analysis as the leadership-time-sink; channel planning + promo design are the levers.
routed_to_questions.evidence: Sales leadership, what you call — you can call it then PNP promotion and planning so much. One big part of trade — accounts and channels. Planning a promotion. Pricing and promotion decision. That takes a lot of time of the leadership level.

routed_to_questions.q_text: Look at the work your team did last week — across the workflow, where is AI today addressing real bottlenecks vs. where would you go next?
routed_to_questions.reasoning: Manu independently validated the thesis B2C/FMCG vs B2B/auto-ancillary cut: variability of demand makes FMCG decision-AI the bigger value pool; auto-ancillary is stable demand, so the value is in physical automation not decision optimization. Direct restatement of the thesis weighting.
routed_to_questions.evidence: If you are in a B2C space, which is more of FMCG and also auto-ancillary supplying B2B — you are supplying to demand, and there's variability there. That makes it easy to disrupt. ... Currently all the automated lines on a car line — you don't shut them and start them, you run at particular speed. The ancillary company is supplying to an auto company; they're truly dependent on a very, very predictable demand. ... In the FMCG sector B2C, it'll be more efficient decisions than humans because of [variability] — that's the difference between B2B and B2C.

routed_to_questions.q_text: Of the 5 FMCG peers you watch most closely, who's furthest ahead on actually deploying AI in operations?
routed_to_questions.reasoning: P&G is on the leading curve among FMCG incumbents — Manu's frame is that the threat isn't peers but upstarts. Tier-1 FMCG mainly competes via change-management speed, not algorithm sophistication. Carve-out: Mamaearth-shape DTC asset-light brands are OUT of the manufacturing-AI thesis.
routed_to_questions.evidence: I think our biggest competitor P&G — like any other big FF [fast-moving] — would be on the leading curve. The challenge would be to bring the people along; you cannot get human out of the loop as fast. The threat would be the new upstarts. If somebody can solve the ESS supplier loop, you can make a company of 10 employees with a billion dollars revenue. ... Two kinds of FMCG: one is the [Mamaearth-shape] of the world — minimal/no manufacturing, everything is contract. There's nothing automated or digitized in between. That is not part of your thesis — manufacturing is not.

routed_to_questions.q_text: What are the parameters used to determine what to outsource versus what to build in-house?
routed_to_questions.reasoning: Concrete model: both ways. External engagement gated by PoC; internal build also active. Adoption mode: small pilots, human-refinement, gradual scale, domain-expert tied in.
routed_to_questions.evidence: It's happening both ways. We went to TCS T-HUB. Our leadership team was there. But we are also engaging external AI companies. We are learning. We are eager. ... Startup engagement model: proof of concept required before enterprise adoption. ... Small pilot projects with human refinement, gradual scaling with domain expert involvement.

routed_to_questions.q_text: Walk me through the implementation reality. AI POCs in last 12–18 months — which graduated to production, which died?
routed_to_questions.reasoning: Most P&G internal AI work has not graduated to production — vision stated, build in progress, but 'not yet there'. Implementation barrier is two-layer: infrastructure/change-management + domain-knowledge mapping.
routed_to_questions.evidence: We're trying. We've been trying. ... Not yet there. But that's where the vision is. ... Digitization needs an infrastructure, needs to think differently. The whole information system and the thought process needs to be changed. The challenge would be to bring the people along.
